B-01.evidence: useUrlSync.ts L80-88: syncToUrl closure calls `serializeGridState(state, keys, prefix, params)` which includes sorting in URL (serializeState.ts L72-89). L69-77 mount effect deserializes sorting from URL → calls hydrateRef.current(partial). Storybook story SortingUrlSync (useUrlSync.stories.tsx L26-113) demonstrates sort toggle button → URL update. vitest S1 (useUrlSync.test.ts L60-78) asserts `url.toContain('sorting=')` after sort change. S4 (L130-153) asserts `onHydrate` called with `{sorting}` partial on mount.

B-02.evidence: useUrlSync.ts L16-25 ALL_KEYS includes 'columnFilters'. Default `keys` covers filter serialization. Storybook (stories.tsx L67-73) toggleFilter button demonstrates `?columnFilters=…` URL reflection. vitest S2 (test.ts L86-99) asserts `url.toContain('columnFilters=')` when both sorting + filter present. S5 (L161-188) asserts filter parsing works even when sibling key (sorting) corrupted.

B-03.evidence: useUrlSync.ts L16-25 ALL_KEYS includes 'pagination'. serializeState.ts L42-52 isDefaultState handles pagination default `{pageIndex:0, pageSize:10}` correctly (returns true to delete key when default). Non-default pagination state is JSON-serialized into URL via L85 `next.set(paramKey, JSON.stringify(value))`. Pagination flows through same state→URL pipeline as sorting (B-01 path) — no special handling required.

B-04.evidence: G-005 is a hook-only utility (no DOM rendering). react-virtual integration unaffected. Spec Section 2.2 confirms `useUrlSync(state, options): void` return type — no UI/DOM component.

B-05.evidence: G-005 scope is URL sync only. No editing functionality. Spec Section 2.1 userJourney has 5 steps all related to URL state sync. EditableGrid/ChangeTrackingGrid are separate modules.
